chunk_summary: Responsive feeding counseling for infants and preschool children with rapid weight gain focuses on structured meals, limiting calorie liquids, avoiding screen-assisted feeding, and promoting sleep and active play to manage overnutrition and prevent obesity persistence.
learning_objectives: Understand the role of feeding patterns in rapid weight gain among children under 5 years.
Learn responsive feeding principles to address overnutrition in outpatient settings.
Identify high-yield feeding issues such as prolonged bottle use, excess milk intake, and screen-assisted feeding.
Implement lifestyle strategies prioritizing structured meals, sleep, and active play over exercise.
key_facts: Rapid upward percentile crossing in children under 5 requires confirmation of height/length accuracy.
Prolonged bottle use, excess milk intake, and sugar-sweetened beverages are common contributors to overnutrition.
Responsive feeding principles: caregivers decide what food is offered and when; children decide how much to eat.
Limit total milk intake to age-appropriate volumes and avoid calorie liquids at bedtime.
Eliminate screen-assisted feeding and sugar-sweetened beverages to improve feeding routines.
reference_quote: Guidelines recommend prioritizing sleep and active play over exercise for children under 5 years of age. (Source: WHO guidelines on physical activity, sedentary behavior, and sleep for children under 5 years)
document_key_topics: Responsive feeding counseling for rapid weight gain
